papers.title_en: FoundationGeo: Learning Spatial Pixel-Wise Fields for Monocular Metric Geometry
papers.authors: Muxin Liu, Xiaoyang Lyu, Tianhe Ren, Peng Dai, Xiaoshan Wu, Zhiyue Zhang, Jiaqi Zhang, Jiehong Lin, Shaoshuai Shi, Xiaojuan Qi
papers.evidence: 单目度量几何估计，像素级场预测
papers.tldr: 针对单目度量几何估计中相对到度量的映射和零样本泛化瓶颈，提出两阶段框架FoundationGeo。第一阶段基于DINOv3训练高保真仿射不变几何模型，第二阶段引入逐像素尺度场和射线方向校正场实现空间校准的度量估计。通过Blender数据引擎合成不同焦距数据以缓解内参分布不匹配。在7个零样本基准上平均超越其他方法5.2%，显著提升跨域鲁棒性。
papers.abstract_en: We present FoundationGeo, a two-stage framework that explicitly bridges relative and metric prediction via spatial calibration and principled data design. Stage 1 learns a high-fidelity, affine-invariant geometry model by initializing with DINOv3 and training on a curated 10.2M-sample multi-domain corpus with complementary local-detail supervision, yielding sharp boundaries and strong cross-domain generalization. Stage 2 moves beyond global scaling by introducing lightweight pixel-wise calibration fields for metric estimation: a scale field for spatially varying metric alignment and a ray-direction correction field that mitigates directional bias in point-map geometry, together producing metrically consistent 3D point maps. Beyond model design, we identify camera intrinsic coverage, especially focal length distribution mismatch between training and test data, as a key bottleneck for zero-shot metric generalization: performance drops sharply when test intrinsics fall outside the training distribution. To address this, we synthesize additional training data across diverse focal lengths using a Blender-based data engine, repairing under-covered focal regimes and improving robustness under intrinsic shift. Extensive zero-shot evaluations across seven benchmarks show that FoundationGeo significantly strengthens cross-domain robustness, staying near the top across diverse domains while avoiding the sharp cross-domain performance drops observed in other methods. This consistency translates into the best overall performance, surpassing heavier baselines by over 5.2% on average.

papers.title_en: FoundationGeo: Learning Spatial Pixel-Wise Fields for Monocular Metric Geometry
papers.authors: Muxin Liu, Xiaoyang Lyu, Tianhe Ren, Peng Dai, Xiaoshan Wu, Zhiyue Zhang, Jiaqi Zhang, Jiehong Lin, Shaoshuai Shi, Xiaojuan Qi
papers.evidence: 单目度量几何估计，像素级场预测
papers.tldr: 单目度量几何估计在零样本跨域场景下泛化能力不足，且全局尺度缩放难以适应空间变化偏差。为此提出FoundationGeo，一个显式桥接相对与度量预测的两阶段框架。阶段1采用DINOv3初始化，在1020万样本多领域语料上训练仿射不变几何模型，附带局部细节监督，获得强泛化能力。阶段2引入轻量级逐像素尺度场和射线方向校正场，分别处理度量对齐和方向偏差。同时，识别焦距分布不匹配是关键瓶颈，通过Blender数据引擎合成多焦距样本修复覆盖。在七个基准上零样本评估，平均超越重基线5.2%，显著增强跨域鲁棒性，避免其他方法的剧烈性能下降。
papers.abstract_en: We present FoundationGeo, a two-stage framework that explicitly bridges relative and metric prediction via spatial calibration and principled data design. Stage 1 learns a high-fidelity, affine-invariant geometry model by initializing with DINOv3 and training on a curated 10.2M-sample multi-domain corpus with complementary local-detail supervision, yielding sharp boundaries and strong cross-domain generalization. Stage 2 moves beyond global scaling by introducing lightweight pixel-wise calibration fields for metric estimation: a scale field for spatially varying metric alignment and a ray-direction correction field that mitigates directional bias in point-map geometry, together producing metrically consistent 3D point maps. Beyond model design, we identify camera intrinsic coverage, especially focal length distribution mismatch between training and test data, as a key bottleneck for zero-shot metric generalization: performance drops sharply when test intrinsics fall outside the training distribution. To address this, we synthesize additional training data across diverse focal lengths using a Blender-based data engine, repairing under-covered focal regimes and improving robustness under intrinsic shift. Extensive zero-shot evaluations across seven benchmarks show that FoundationGeo significantly strengthens cross-domain robustness, staying near the top across diverse domains while avoiding the sharp cross-domain performance drops observed in other methods. This consistency translates into the best overall performance, surpassing heavier baselines by over 5.2% on average.

papers.title_en: Physics-Informed Diffusion for Biomechanically Plausible 3D Sign Language Generation
papers.authors: Emanuele Colonna, Moises Diaz, Gennaro Vessio, Miguel Angel Ferrer, Giovanna Castellano
papers.evidence: 物理信息扩散模型用于3D骨骼运动生成
papers.tldr: 手语生成需同时满足语义保真和生物力学合理性，现有方法将骨架视为无结构向量导致骨长漂移、关节角违规等问题。提出PIDiffSign，一种物理信息扩散模型，通过可微几何模块和多种解剖约束融入扩散过程。在PHOENIX14T和CSL-Daily数据集上，该方法在姿态精度、关节角正确性、分布真实性和反向翻译质量上显著优于强基线。结果表明物理信息扩散可同时提升运动真实感和语义保真，为手语生成提供了新范式。
papers.abstract_en: Sign language production, which generates continuous 3D skeletal motion from spoken language input, must simultaneously satisfy two constraints: semantic fidelity, so that a deaf viewer can recognize the intended sequence of glosses, and biomechanical plausibility, so that the generated skeleton respects anatomical constraints. Existing approaches optimize semantic reconstruction through coordinate-based objectives that treat the skeleton as an unstructured vector, thus allowing for bone length drift, joint angle violations, and temporarily locked fingers. We introduce PIDiffSign, a physics-informed diffusion model for gloss-to-pose translation that incorporates anatomical constraints into both the architecture and training objective. The model uses a Transformer encoder-decoder, where the decoder is conditioned on the diffusion time step through adaptive zero-initialized layer normalization and cross-attends to gloss representations. A differentiable geometry module enforces bone length consistency and biologically valid joint angles throughout generation. Training combines anthropomorphic, kinematic, angular, and finger-joint constraints with a contrastive gloss-pose alignment loss and classifier-free guidance for semantically conditioned sampling. Experiments on the PHOENIX14T and CSL-Daily benchmarks show consistent improvements over a strong diffusion baseline in pose accuracy, joint-angle correctness, distributional realism, and back-translation quality. These results demonstrate that physics-informed diffusion improves both motion realism and semantic fidelity for sign language generation.

papers.title_en: DynTrace: Tracking Dynamic Object Evidence for 4D Spatio-Temporal Reasoning in MLLMs
papers.authors: Rongxin Gao, Yuzhi Huang, Dongxuan Liu, Chu Li, Zhenye Wang, Jie Wu, Shuzhao Xie, Jingyan Jiang, Xinghao Ding, Xiaotong Tu, Yue Huang
papers.evidence: 用于动态场景理解的4D时空推理
papers.tldr: 针对多模态大模型在4D时空推理中因依赖稀疏帧导致动态物体感知碎片化、无法区分物体运动与相机运动的问题，提出无训练框架DynTrace。通过动态轨迹可视化将世界坐标轨迹重投影到图像平面，提供几何先验；同时构建动态痕迹图追踪物体级动态线索。方法一致提升开源模型性能，在Dyn-Bench、VLM4D、DSI-Bench上达到最优，验证了连续动态证据对鲁棒4D推理的关键作用。
papers.abstract_en: 4D spatio-temporal reasoning, jointly modeling 3D spatial structure and temporal evolution, is essential for understanding dynamic worlds and enabling embodied interaction. While current Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) show strong capabilities in static scene understanding and coarse-grained 4D tasks, they still have notable limitations in continuous dynamic scene perception, especially in tracking dynamic object evidence for coherent 4D spatio-temporal reasoning. This shortcoming stems mainly from relying on sparse frame-level observations, fragmenting continuous dynamic cues and leaving models unable to disentangle genuine object dynamics from camera-induced apparent motion. Inspired by humans tracking dynamic cues while compensating for viewpoint changes, we propose DynTrace, a training-free framework for 4D spatio-temporal reasoning with two complementary components. Dynamic Trajectory Visualization (DTV) reprojects world-coordinate trajectories onto the image plane, providing geometry-informed visual priors that disentangle genuine object dynamics from camera-induced apparent motion. Meanwhile, the Dynamic Trace Token (DT-Token), organized into a Dynamic Trace Graph (DTG), tracks object-level dynamic cues, trace evolution, and key moments, maintaining continuous dynamic object evidence for coherent 4D reasoning. Together, these two components equip MLLMs with continuously tracked dynamic object evidence, grounded in geometry-informed visual priors and structured spatio-temporal traces. DynTrace consistently improves open-source MLLMs, achieving state-of-the-art results on Dyn-Bench, VLM4D, and DSI-Bench, validating the importance of tracking dynamic object evidence for robust 4D spatio-temporal reasoning.

papers.title_en: FoundationGeo: Learning Spatial Pixel-Wise Fields for Monocular Metric Geometry
papers.authors: Muxin Liu, Xiaoyang Lyu, Tianhe Ren, Peng Dai, Xiaoshan Wu, Zhiyue Zhang, Jiaqi Zhang, Jiehong Lin, Shaoshuai Shi, Xiaojuan Qi
papers.evidence: 学习空间逐像素场以进行单目度量3D几何
papers.tldr: 单目度量几何面临从相对深度到绝对度量的转换挑战，且现有方法在零样本泛化时因相机内参分布不匹配导致性能骤降。FoundationGeo提出两阶段框架：阶段1利用DINOv3初始化并在大规模多域数据上训练高保真仿射不变几何模型；阶段2引入轻量像素级标定场（尺度场和射线方向校正场）实现空间变化度量对齐，同时通过Blender数据引擎合成多焦距训练数据以修复内参覆盖不足。在7个零样本基准上，该方法显著提升跨域鲁棒性，平均性能超越最重基线5.2%以上，为单目度量几何提供了高效且稳健的解决方案。
papers.abstract_en: We present FoundationGeo, a two-stage framework that explicitly bridges relative and metric prediction via spatial calibration and principled data design. Stage 1 learns a high-fidelity, affine-invariant geometry model by initializing with DINOv3 and training on a curated 10.2M-sample multi-domain corpus with complementary local-detail supervision, yielding sharp boundaries and strong cross-domain generalization. Stage 2 moves beyond global scaling by introducing lightweight pixel-wise calibration fields for metric estimation: a scale field for spatially varying metric alignment and a ray-direction correction field that mitigates directional bias in point-map geometry, together producing metrically consistent 3D point maps. Beyond model design, we identify camera intrinsic coverage, especially focal length distribution mismatch between training and test data, as a key bottleneck for zero-shot metric generalization: performance drops sharply when test intrinsics fall outside the training distribution. To address this, we synthesize additional training data across diverse focal lengths using a Blender-based data engine, repairing under-covered focal regimes and improving robustness under intrinsic shift. Extensive zero-shot evaluations across seven benchmarks show that FoundationGeo significantly strengthens cross-domain robustness, staying near the top across diverse domains while avoiding the sharp cross-domain performance drops observed in other methods. This consistency translates into the best overall performance, surpassing heavier baselines by over 5.2% on average.

papers.title_en: UniPose9D: Universal Category-Agnostic Object Pose Estimation
papers.authors: Yang You, Yi Du, Cole Harrison, Leonidas Guibas
papers.evidence: 通用类别3D物体姿态估计
papers.tldr: 现有物体姿态估计方法过度拟合基准，泛化到新类别和场景的能力有限。针对此问题，提出UniPose9D，一个类别无关的9D姿态估计基础模型，仅需实例掩码和深度信息，无需类别标签或CAD模型。其核心是采样点对并利用DINOv2和PointNet特征预测NOCS坐标，再通过点对RANSAC N-hop Kabsch-Umeyama算法估计姿态，并引入流匹配处理对称性。在六个数据集上的实验表明，单一模型可匹配甚至超越专门方法，且能泛化到未见物体。贡献包括构建大规模训练集并开源代码。
papers.abstract_en: Object pose estimation is a fundamental problem in 3D vision. Although recent state-of-the-art approaches achieve strong performance, they often overfit to existing benchmarks and exhibit limited generalization to novel categories and unseen scenes. We propose UniPose9D, a category-agnostic foundation model for 9D object pose estimation: given an instance mask/ROI and either an RGB-D observation or an RGB image with predicted depth, the model estimates rotation, translation, and metric size without category labels, CAD models, mean-shape priors, or reference views. Specifically, UniPose9D samples point pairs from the observed object geometry and uses DINOv2 and PointNet features to predict NOCS coordinates for each pair. To improve accuracy, we introduce a point-pair-based RANSAC N-hop Kabsch--Umeyama algorithm with an adaptive threshold. We further employ flow matching to address symmetric ambiguities and construct a large-scale training set by curating and aligning pose annotations from existing public datasets. Experiments across six datasets show that a single unified model can match or surpass specialist methods while generalizing to unseen objects and in-the-wild scenarios. Our code and model are available on https://github.com/qq456cvb/UniPose9D.

papers.title_en: When Depth Is Better Told Than Shown: Depth-Ordinal Prompting for Vision-Language Spatial Reasoning
papers.authors: Quynh Vo, Phuc Dao, Cong-Duy Nguyen, Thong Nguyen
papers.evidence: 视觉语言模型中的空间推理用于3D理解
papers.tldr: 视觉语言模型在空间推理中表现欠佳，直接输入深度图反而因噪声干扰降低性能。研究发现深度信息虽已存在于语言模型中但难以提取。为此提出无需训练的Depth-Ordinal Prompting（DOP）方法，将单目深度转化为针对查询对象的序数文本提示。实验表明DOP在可靠物体级排序下有效提升推理准确率，在强原始图像场景保持中性，揭示了深度信号以图像形式呈现有害而以文本形式呈现有利的关键发现。
papers.abstract_en: Vision-language models (VLMs) are expected to reason about physical space -- which object is closer, what lies behind what, and how objects are arranged in 3D -- yet they still struggle with such spatial judgments. A natural remedy is to show the model a depth map, but we find that this can make performance worse. We show that depth is not absent: it reaches the language model, but becomes difficult to access for downstream reasoning, while rendered pseudo-depth maps act as noisy auxiliary images that frozen VLMs cannot easily regulate. We propose Depth-Ordinal Prompting (DOP), a training-free method that converts monocular depth into a single question-targeted ordinal text cue at the queried objects, without adding a depth image, training a module, injecting features, or using labels. Our key finding is form dependence: the same depth signal can hurt when shown as an image but help when told as text.Across benchmarks, models, and depth estimators, DOP improves spatial reasoning when pseudo-depth provides reliable object-level ordering and remains largely neutral in strong original-image regimes. It is also competitive with the strongest training-free depth-prompting alternative while being simpler and more targeted.

papers.title_en: HyperShadow: A Benchmark for Detecting 3D Projections of Higher-Dimensional Spatial Objects
papers.evidence: 检测高维物体3D投影的基准，涉及3D形状理解
papers.tldr: 现有机器学习数据集中的“4D”实为3D+时间，缺乏真正高维空间物体的三维投影检测基准。本文提出HyperShadow，判断3D点云是原生三维形状还是来自四到六维空间刚性物体的投影（影子）。利用投影签名、密度折叠等特征，190k参数点网络达96.6%准确率，泛化至79-91%；针对旋转序列引入零参数刚性见证（Kabsch对齐残差），AUROC达0.982。该基准为区分纯三维与高维投影提供了可重复工具。
papers.abstract_en: Machine-learning datasets labelled "4D" universally denote three spatial dimensions plus time. We introduce HyperShadow, the first public benchmark in which the fourth, fifth, and sixth dimensions are spatial: the task is to decide whether a 3D point cloud is a native three-dimensional shape or the projection, the "shadow", of a rigid object living in R^N (N = 4-6). We show this task is fundamentally distinct from intrinsic-dimension estimation: a shadow is still at-most-3-dimensional data, and standard estimators (TwoNN, Levina-Bickel MLE) reach only 71-73% accuracy. Detection instead requires projection signatures, density folds, filled volumes with characteristic radial profiles, and topology changes, which a 190k-parameter point network recovers at 96.6% accuracy across four corruption tiers, generalizing at 79-91% to object families never seen in training. On a temporal track of rigidly rotating objects we introduce a zero-parameter rigidity witness: the residual of the optimal rigid 3D alignment (Kabsch) between consecutive frames, which must vanish for any rigid 3D motion but cannot vanish for the shadow of a rigid rotation in R^N. This single interpretable statistic separates the classes at AUROC 0.982. All data are generated reproducibly from seeds; the dataset, models, and code are released publicly. HyperShadow makes no claim about physical reality; it is a controlled instrument for studying which observable statistics can certify incompatibility with a purely three-dimensional explanation.
